task: I’ve got a delivery that may slip because a file issue set us back, and I need to tell the client in a way that’s clear without making it worse. Can you help me draft a short message that owns the problem, gives a realistic update, and keeps the tone calm?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a calm, short version:

> Hi [Client Name] — I ran into a file issue that’s set the delivery back a bit. I’m working through it now and focusing on getting the file stable before I send anything out. I expect to have an updated version to you by [time/day].  

If you want it a touch firmer:

> Hi [Client Name] — I hit a file issue that delayed the delivery, and I’m resolving it now. I’m prioritizing the fix and will send the updated version by [time/day].

If you want, I can also make it:
- more apologetic
- more neutral
- more specific about the next step

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close. But I’d want it a little more specific about what I’m actually doing, and less like I’m just “working through it.” That can sound vague.

Maybe:

> Hi [Client Name] — I ran into a file issue that’s delayed delivery. I’m fixing it now and verifying the export before I send it, so I don’t hand off anything unstable. I expect to have the updated file to you by [time/day].

If you want, make it a bit firmer / more client-facing, but still calm.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a firmer, still calm version:

> Hi [Client Name] — I ran into a file issue that delayed delivery. I’m fixing it now and verifying the export before I send it, so I don’t hand off anything unstable. I expect to have the updated file to you by [time/day].

If you want it slightly more client-facing and direct:

> Hi [Client Name] — I hit a file issue that delayed delivery. I’m resolving it now and checking the export before sending, so the version you get is stable and ready to review. I expect to send the updated file by [time/day].

If you want, I can also give you:
- a more **neutral** version
- a more **own-the-problem** version
- a **shorter email/Slack** version

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically it. I’d want the “own the problem” version, but tighter — less corporate, more plainspoken. Something like:

> Hi [Client Name] — I hit a file issue that delayed delivery. I’m fixing it now and checking the export before I send it, so I don’t pass along anything unstable. I expect to send the updated file by [time/day].

If you can make it a little less repetitive around “fixing/checking/sending,” that’d be better.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, plainspoken version:

> Hi [Client Name] — I hit a file issue that delayed delivery. I’m sorting it out now and will verify the export before I send anything over. I expect to have the updated file to you by [time/day].

If you want it a touch firmer:

> Hi [Client Name] — I hit a file issue that delayed delivery. I’m resolving it now and will verify the export before I send the updated file by [time/day].

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, plain enough. The first one reads best.
